name: Grajal de Campos from Sahagún – circular by the río Cea
name_original: Grajal de Campos desde Sahagún – circular por el río Cea
region_intro: Hiking around Sahagún offers routes through the flat, agricultural plains of the Tierra de Campos region in León, Spain. The landscape is characterized by open fields, river valleys, and historical pilgrimage paths, notably the Camino Francés. The area features gentle elevation changes, with the Río Cea often accompanying the trails. Best hiking trails around Sahagún The most popular hiking route is Camino Francés: Moratinos to Ermita de la Virgen del Puente, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This moderate path follows a section of the historic Camino Francés through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Grajal de Campos from Sahagún – circular by the río Cea, a moderate 10.5 miles (17.0 km) path. This circular route offers views along the Río Cea and through agricultural areas. Local hikers also love the Camino Francés: Sahagún Loop, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through the immediate surroundings of Sahagún, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Sahagún is defined by river paths, agricultural plains, and sections of the historic Camino Francés. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging day hikes. The routes in Sahagún are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Sahagún's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sahagún?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes documented around Sahagún on komoot, offering a variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Sahagún?
A: Yes, Sahagún offers 5 easy hiking trails. These routes typically feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, making them ideal for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176694088/camino-frances-sahagun-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Sahagún Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.2-mile (9.9 km) trail that is often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes, offering a pleasant walk through the immediate surroundings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sahagún?
A: The best time for hiking in Sahagún is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are mild and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winters are typically cold but usually without heavy snow, making them suitable for hiking if properly equipped.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on the trails around Sahagún?
A: Most trails around Sahagún, especially those through rural and agricultural areas, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through farmlands or near livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Sahagún?
A: Yes, Sahagún features several circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176660501/grajal-de-campos-from-sahagun-circular-by-the-rio-cea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grajal de Campos from Sahagún – circular by the río Cea&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.5-mile (17.0 km) path that offers scenic views along the Río Cea and through agricultural areas.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on hikes around Sahagún?
A: Hiking around Sahagún offers routes through the flat, agricultural plains of the Tierra de Campos region. You'll experience open fields, river valleys, and sections of the historic Camino Francés. The landscape is characterized by gentle elevation changes, with the Río Cea often accompanying the trails.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Sahagún pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/916337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geographical Center of the Camino Francés – Ermita de la Virgen del Puente&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4441511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Benito Arch and Ruins of the Monastery of San Benito&lt;/a&gt;, or even the traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bodegas de Moratinos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging hiking trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there are 3 difficult hiking routes around Sahagún. These trails may involve longer distances or more varied terrain, providing a more strenuous experience for seasoned hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sahagún?
A: The routes in Sahagún are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 17 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural landscapes, the historical significance of the Camino Francés sections, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Camino Francés?
A: Many routes in Sahagún incorporate sections of the historic Camino Francés. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102646641/camino-frances-moratinos-to-ermita-de-la-virgen-del-puente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Moratinos to Ermita de la Virgen del Puente&lt;/a&gt; is a 9.4-mile (15.2 km) trail that follows a significant portion of this pilgrimage path through rural landscapes.

Q: What should I bring for a hike around Sahagún?
A: Given the open and agricultural nature of the terrain, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and comfortable hiking shoes. Layers of clothing are also recommended as temperatures can change, especially during spring and autumn. A map or GPS device is useful for navigation, though many trails are well-marked.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Sahagún?
A: In Sahagún and surrounding villages, parking is generally available in public areas or near the start points of trails. As many routes pass through or start from villages, you can often find street parking. For specific trailheads, it's best to check the individual route details on komoot for precise parking information.
tips: The Arco de San Benito is an impressive monument in Sahagún, located in the region of Castilla y León, Spain. It was built around 1662 by the architect Felipe Berrojo to replace the old Romanesque portal of the Monasterio de San Benito. The arch is designed as a triumphal arch, under which the N-120 passes, and is also known as "la puerta de Sahagún" (the gate of Sahagún). The monument is richly decorated with a central shield, inscriptions and sculptures of Saints Facundo and Primitivo. At the top is the royal shield, and there are also sculptures of Alfonso III and Alfonso VI, who were important protectors of the monastery.
National heritage monument, the Sanctuary of the Pilgrim of Sahagún on the Camino de Santiago. Do not forget to get your Camino card when you pass through the Sanctuary.

Amidst golden undulating plains, the imposing Castle of Grajal de Campos, from the 16th century, is considered the first artillery castle in Spain. It serves as an omen of the beautiful town that we are going to find, which is usually overlooked among those who do not leave the Camino.
Great walk along the Cea river near Sahagún. Recreational walk green lunch. Just listen to the water and the singing of the birds.
